notes: [cl-hud.engine.registry_and_order | presentation] Panel registry, z-order, panel-order cvar  -  Draw order follows Godot child order, not the QC _hud_panelorder reverse-walk; relative panel layering may differ but panels don't overlap in luma.

[cl-hud.panel.radar | presentation,liveness] Radar / minimap panel (#6)  -  Maximized clickable mode (ons spawn / teleport select) is still unported; the discovered radar panel is force-hidden (StartHiddenIds) to avoid a double radar and the live radar is the standalone _radar.
[cl-hud.panel.pressedkeys | presentation] Pressed-keys panel (#11)  -  key_*/key_*_inv per-key art coverage not all runtime-verified (the inverted/pressed art set).

[cl-hud.panel.infomessages | logic] Info-messages panel (#14)  -  Cycling group hints (spectate controls), team-unbalanced nag, spectator list and the full message set are partially covered (the eliminated-LMS line is now present).
[cl-hud.panel.itemstime | presentation] Items-time panel (#17)  -  Progressbar / dynamicsize / ratio-columns fidelity not runtime-verified.
[cl-hud.panel.quickmenu | logic,presentation] Quick-menu panel  -  Nested quick-chat tree from a quickmenu file + translatecommands/server_is_default parity not verified.
[cl-hud.panel.strafehud | presentation] Strafe HUD panel  -  Bar zones / best-angle marker / wturn-switch indicator + mode/style/range fidelity not runtime-verified.

[cl-hud.panel.minigame | logic,presentation] Minigame HUD panels (BOARD/STATUS/HELP/MENU)  -  Board + status are merged into one MinigameRenderer (board square + team pieces + win-line + status line) rather than the QC BOARD/STATUS split; only the implemented minigame rule-sets are renderable. Per-game _hud_board/_hud_status art coverage not all runtime-verified.

[cl-hud.draw.circle_clipped_pic | presentation] Radial ring clipping primitive (DrawCircleClippedPic)  -  QC draws additively (DRAWFLAG_ADDITIVE); the port's immediate-mode DrawColoredPolygon is alpha-blended (documented in-code as reading fine at the ring's low alphas).; Geometry is a segment fan rather than the QC 1-4 quadrant polygons — same swept wedge, different tessellation (visual difference at most at the wedge edge).

[cl-hud.draw.pic_tiled | logic,presentation,liveness] Repeat-tiling pic primitive (drawpic_tiled)  -  No tiling draw primitive exists in the port (no DrawTextureRect tile:true HUD use). The scoreboard's tiled gfx/scoreboard/scoreboard_bg row backing is absent entirely (no 'scoreboard_bg' art reference in game/); the configure-mode panel-highlight tiled border art is approximated by a flat 2px frame (documented at HudConfigEditor.cs:1250).

[cl-hud.draw.string_aspect | logic,presentation] Fit-to-box text scaling (drawstring_aspect / DRAWSTRING_ASPECT_SCALE + expanding text variants)  -  No aspect-fit text primitive: where Base shrinks an over-long string to its box and recenters (drawstring_aspect users: timer/score/physics/vote/engineinfo/ammo values, gametype modicon counts), the port draws at a precomputed size — long strings can clip/overflow instead of shrinking.; The expanding (fadelerp-driven) TEXT variants (drawstring_expanding / drawcolorcodedstring_*_expanding, used by score pop-ins) are unported; only the PIC expanding variant is (PowerupsPanel expandingbox — see cl-hud.panel.powerups).

[cl-hud.header.mapvote | logic,liveness] Mapvote client header contract (highlight border, input event, picture net download)  -  MapVote_InputEvent (mouse/keyboard cell selection casting the vote) is not wired — votes ride impulses only (already tracked in cl-hud.panel.mapvote).; Net_MapVote_Picture + cl_readpicture_force (the server-pushed ballot level-shot download protocol) is unported (no port hits); the port reads local levelshots via the VFS only, so a client without the map's pk3 sees the nopreview fallback where Base would stream the picture.

[cl-hud.header.weapons | logic,values,liveness] Weapons panel header defaults (weapons.qh) + accuracy color-level table  -  hud_panel_weapons_orderbyimpulse: wrong default (0 vs Base 1) AND dead — nothing reads it, so the grid is never impulse-ordered where Base defaults to impulse order.; Accuracy_LoadLevels/Accuracy_GetColor's accuracy_color_levels + accuracy_color<N> cvar table is unported; both WeaponsPanel.AccuracyColor and ScoreboardPanel.AccuracyColor use a hardcoded ramp (user color-level customization is a no-op; default look approximates the shipped 0..100 red→green).; cl_weaponpriority is used by the menu weapon-order editor (DialogSettingsGame/WeaponOrder) but its effect on this panel's non-impulse sort order was not traced.

[cl-hud.header.racetimer | logic,presentation,liveness] Race HUD client state block (racetimer.qh race_* globals)  -  The header's race_* wire-state block (race_checkpoint_splits[256], race_status/_name, race_myrank, othercheckpoint enemy deltas) is replaced by a host-side Race-state feed — no TE_CSQC_RACE consumer, so a pure --connect client has none of it (tracked in cl-hud.panel.racetimer/checkpoints).; Qualifying penalty display state (race_penaltyaccumulator/penaltyeventtime/penaltytime/penaltyreason) and the speed-award fields (race_speedaward*, alltimebest) were not located in the port — unknown, likely missing.

[cl-hud.header.quickmenu | logic,values,liveness] Quickmenu header defaults + file-source cvars (quickmenu.qh)  -  The file-sourced quickmenu tree is unported: hud_panel_quickmenu_file and the server-pushed _hud_panel_quickmenu_file_from_server have no port reader, so only the built-in default tree exists (sharpens the existing cl-hud.panel.quickmenu 'entry-source fidelity' gap into a confirmed missing).

[cl-hud.header.powerups | values,presentation] Powerups header defaults + powerup progressbar color cvars (powerups.qh)  -  Powerup colors are WRONG vs Base: Base's strength/shield/superweapons addPowerupItem calls pass the hud_progressbar_*_color cvars (orange/green/yellow-ish), but PowerupsPanel hardcodes the QC entity m_color colormods (blue/magenta/white) on the mistaken premise (its own :141-144 comment) that Base uses m_color — HudConfig registers the correct cvar values (HudConfig.cs:53-54) yet the panel never reads them (superweapons color isn't registered at all). Speed/invisibility buffs DO use m_color in Base and match.

[cl-hud.header.infomessages | values,presentation] Infomessages header defaults (infomessages.qh flip/dynamichud/cl_showspectators)  -  Base pins the infomessages panel still during HUD shake/sway (dynamichud false, both the .qh default and _hud_common.cfg:66); the port's whole-layer dynamic transform moves it (see cl-hud.draw.hud_shift_scale_macros).; cl_showspectators (spectator-list toggle the header declares) was not located in the port — the spectator list is already flagged partial on cl-hud.panel.infomessages.

[cl-strafehud.input.player_state | logic,liveness] Strafeplayer state acquisition: onground/jump-held/waterlevel/onslick + ground timeout for slick ramps  -  JumpHeld/OnSlick are properties that NO live caller sets (NetGame only sets Player) — jump-held ground-flicker suppression and the slick ground-accel switch never engage in a real match.; Waterlevel is never determined (Base _Movetype_CheckWater probe): swimming never resets onground_lasttime and never suppresses the jump-height capture.; The onslick ground trace (tracebox 1qu down, Q3SURFACEFLAG_SLICK) is not performed anywhere on the panel's behalf.; Spectator/demo path missing: Base resolves the spectated player via CSQCModel_server2csqc and keeps working; port draws only for the local listen-server Player and self-blanks on a pure client (LocalServerPlayer null) — the panel never renders in network-client play.

[cl-strafehud.input.wishangle_keys | logic,liveness] Wish-angle + forward/backward key derivation (local movement vector vs non-local pressed-keys fallback)  -  Local branch is ported faithfully but is DEAD live: WishDir is never fed, so isLocal is always false and the panel always runs the fallback.; Non-local fallback diverges: Base still reads STAT(PRESSED_KEYS) (45°/90° ± left/right sign, 0 when no side key); port hardcodes wishangle=45, keysFwd=FORWARD — pure-A/D side strafing and backwards strafing are misclassified on the live path.

[cl-strafehud.input.phys_cvars | logic,values] Movement-physics input surface (PHYS_* stat reads -> maxspeed/accel/friction/aircontrol/etc.)  -  PhysSlickAccelerate() maps to the wrong cvar (sv_airaccelerate instead of sv_slickaccelerate; sv_slickaccelerate IS in the port's MoveVarsBlock/PhysicsPreset) — slick-ground zone angles use accel 2 instead of 15.; PhysAirStopAccelerateFull() maps to sv_aircontrol==0 instead of sv_airstopaccelerate_full (also present in MoveVarsBlock).; Base PHYS_* are per-entity stats: haste/speed-mutator/gametype modifiers and crouch (0.5) reach the HUD; the port's raw global cvar reads never reflect per-player modifiers.

[cl-strafehud.math.frametime | logic,values,timing] Frame-time estimator (weighted-mean of input_timelength, 50ms DP frame split, fps_update window)  -  Port always returns ticrate (the Base spectate branch). The predicted-player path — weighted arithmetic mean (sum dt² / sum dt) refreshed every fps_update seconds with the 50 ms split — is not ported, so maxaccel*dt (and therefore bestspeed and every zone angle) is computed for the server tick, not the client's real frame cadence.

[cl-strafehud.math.friction_replica | values] On-ground friction-speed replica (geometric per-tick friction, stopspeed cases)  -  dt_r constant wrong (1/60 vs 1/256): independent_geometric = (1 - friction*dt_r)^(dt/dt_r) and the stopspeed-band log term both use the wrong base, so frictionspeed/strafespeed — and every ground zone angle derived from them — deviate from Base for all on-ground frames.

[cl-strafehud.math.zone_angles | values] Best/prebest/overturn angle math (ground-friction, airborne, high-speed-landing onground_mode branches)  -  Formula structure is faithful, but the inputs are tainted upstream: wrong friction-replica dt_r (see math.friction_replica), wrong slick accel + no ducked mod (input.phys_cvars), ticrate dt (math.frametime) — so the drawn angles differ from Base numerically even though the algebra matches.

[cl-strafehud.math.turn_sidestrafe | liveness] Air side-strafe (A/D turn) detection: strafity, GeomLerp maxspeed/maxaccel blends, timeout_turn retention  -  Reachable only with strafekeys (|wishangle|>45) — which requires the never-fed WishDir; on the live path wishangle is pinned at 45 so side-strafe mode NEVER activates (turn stays false, strafity stays 0, range_sidestrafe never engages).

[cl-strafehud.math.wturn | liveness] W-turn best-angle (aircontrol power==2 cubic, acos(sqrt(2/3)) asymptote, proper/unrestricted modes)  -  wturning requires wishangle==0 && keysFwd==FORWARD-ish — live feed pins wishangle to 45/keysFwd to FORWARD (non-local fallback), so wturning is never true and the wturn indicators only appear via the WTURN_NORMAL/SIDESTRAFE always-modes, with dt = ticrate skewing the proper-formula variant.; PhysAirAccelQw gate reads sv_airaccel_qw as a global (stock -0.8 -> gate correctly closed by default), but PhysAirControlPenalty/Power/Flags are also raw globals (no per-player stats).

[cl-strafehud.bar.projection_range | values] Projection modes (linear/perspective/panoramic) + HUD angle range resolution (range / range_sidestrafe / FOV modes)  -  range -1 / range_sidestrafe -1 ('use current FOV') modes use the never-fed FovX default of 90 — wrong whenever the player's fov/zoom differs from 90 (grep: no assignment to strafe.FovX anywhere in game/).; range_sidestrafe GeomLerp path is unreachable live because strafity is pinned 0 (see math.turn_sidestrafe).

[cl-strafehud.extra.slick_detector | logic,presentation,liveness] Slick detector (hemispherical trace fan below the player, top/bottom warning bars)  -  The detector's substance — the traceline fan (90°/2^granularity steps over a hemisphere of radius slickdetector_range, Q3SURFACEFLAG_SLICK check) — is not implemented; the port only displays a fed OnSlick flag that no live caller sets. Warning bars can therefore only ever appear on an all-slick (sv_friction 0) map.; The reserved text-offset height IS returned when the cvar gate passes (matching Base), so text placement is unaffected.

[cl-strafehud.extra.text_indicators | values,presentation,liveness] Fading text readouts: race start speed, jump height, vertical angle, strafe efficiency (+ unit conversion)  -  Start-speed capture is dead live: RaceCheckpointTime/RaceStartSpeed are never fed (the RaceTimer panel gets its own feed but nobody mirrors it to the strafehud), so the default-on startspeed readout never displays.; hud_speed_unit is ignored: the SpeedUnit property stays 1 (qu), so unit selection via the real cvar (which PhysicsPanel honors) does not affect strafehud text.; Jump-height capture runs but 'swimming' is hardcoded false (no waterlevel probe) and the start-latch resets rely on Player-only data; the Base start-trigger latch GATE (race_nextcheckpoint==1 || checkpoint 254/nextcheckpoint 255, extra.qc:191) is un-ported entirely — the port latches on ANY RaceCheckpointTime change (StrafeHudPanel.cs:1390),

[cl-strafehud.audio.sonar | logic,values,timing,audio] Strafe sonar (audible strafe-quality beeps: interval/volume/pitch ramp by strafe ratio)  -  Entire feature absent: no sonar code, none of the 13 hud_panel_strafehud_sonar_* cvars registered, no sound path/precache handling. Default-off in Base, but racers who enable it get nothing.

[cl-scoreboard.field.formatting | logic,presentation] Per-field value formatting (ScoreString/frags/kdr/sum/dmg)  -  SP_FPS and SP_SKILL columns never render (FieldByLabel returns null for fps/skill — client-only display fields with no port plumbing); the GetField FPS/SKILL branches exist (ScoreboardPanel.cs:684-697) but are unreachable because the columns are never fed.; scores_per_round averaging is now plumbed (ScoresPerRound + per-field roundsPlayed division), but no interactive scores_per_round toggle (Ctrl+R) — only a console cvar drives it.

[cl-scoreboard.field.name_icons | logic,values,timing,presentation,liveness] Name-cell icons (player color / ready / handicap / ignored / wants-join)  -  Handicap icon is PORTED + LIVE (DrawRow:1075-1083 draws a square at the EXACT QC tint '1 0 0' + '0 1 1' * ((16-lvl)/15), white@1 → red@16, fed by the networked handicap_level).; Still missing: player-color shirt/pants swatch (gfx/scoreboard/playercolor_*), pulsing ready check, ignored icon, the pulsing (Q) wants-join team marker, and the playerid '#N' prefix (Scoreboard_AddPlayerId).; Handicap icon is drawn as a flat colored square (no gfx/scoreboard/player_handicap texture); presentation faithful in tint but not in the actual icon art.

[cl-scoreboard.teams.grouping_totals | values,presentation] Team sections + per-team totals  -  Only the primary team slot total is shown (SetWireRows stores wire.Teams primary slot); the secondary teamscore (e.g. keyhunt) is not networked/rendered (the wire's Teams list carries a single (team, score) pair).; team_size_position N/M side display is now PORTED (DrawTeamTotals:934-962, 0/1/2 placement), but totals are still drawn as colored banner boxes rather than the QC bold hud_fontsize*1.5 free-standing side numbers; size string uses 12px text on the box.

[cl-scoreboard.header.gameinfo | presentation] Game-info header (next map / gametype banner / limits / map+players)  -  Lead-limit header term IS now built (BuildLimitsHeader handles ll > 0 && (ll < fl || fl <= 0) with the '^2+N' term and the '& '/'/'  delimiter, LeadLimit/LeadAndFragLimit fed from cvars NetGame.cs:4175-4176).; m_hidelimits and campaign player-count suppression not handled.

[cl-scoreboard.block.spectators | presentation] Spectator list  -  spectators_position (0/1/2/3 placement around the blocks) and spectators_aligned column alignment not implemented (drawn after the table only).

[cl-scoreboard.block.accuracy | logic,values,timing,presentation] Accuracy stats block  -  Drawn as plain text '%' cells, not the per-weapon ICON grid (drawpic weapon model2) with column/row highlighting.; No accuracy_showdelay gating, no warmup suppression, no doublerows, no Accuracy_GetColor weapon ramp or nocolors option; the port's average color ramp differs from QC's per-weapon ramp.

[cl-scoreboard.block.itemstats | logic,values,timing,presentation] Item-stats block  -  No item-stats block at all: Base shows a per-item icon grid of pickup counts (g_inventory.inv_items), filtered by itemstats_filter_mask=12. The port has no SetItemStats / DrawItemStats and inventory is not networked for it.

[cl-scoreboard.block.rankings | presentation] Race/CTS rankings block (+ speed award)  -  Speed-award / all-time-fastest line is now PORTED + LIVE (DrawSpeedAward, ScoreboardPanel.cs:1257, fed by SetSpeedAward NetGame.cs:3837 from networked SpeedAward/Best + holders; QC unit conversion + alltimebest gate).; No multi-column wrap / horizontal scroll (single-column rankings list).

[cl-scoreboard.block.mapstats | liveness] Map stats (monsters / secrets)  -  Map-stats are read from server-side static state (MonsterAI / MapObjectsState) directly on the client feed path — live for a listen-server host, but neither monsters nor secrets are networked as a STAT, so a remote (non-host) client would not see the rows.

[cl-scoreboard.ui.interactive | logic,values,timing,presentation] Interactive scoreboard UI (navigation / team select / spectate / kick / tell / column cycling)  -  No interactive UI: the port scoreboard is a passive overlay. Missing TAB panel cycling (SCOREBOARD↔RANKINGS), arrow-key player/team selection, ENTER/SPACE team-join + spectate, Ctrl+C column-layout cycle, Ctrl+R scores_per_round toggle, Ctrl+T tell, Ctrl+K vote-kick, ESC exit, and the team-selection screen (scoreboard_ui_enabled==2) with its join prompts and world dimming (0.7).

[cl-scoreboard.draw.export | logic,presentation] Scoreboard text export (dump_scoreboard / scoreboard_export)  -  No equivalent of Scoreboard_Draw_Export (writes the scoreboard table to a file handle for the dump-scoreboard console command); the port has no scoreboard-export command.

[onslaught.cp.touch_build | presentation] Control-point capture-by-build (touch → icon → build → flip)  -  Presentation still missing: no EFFECT_CAP/EFFECT_RAGE particles, no pad-model swap (MDL_ONS_CP_PAD1/2), no build-bar waypoint sprite.; Audio emitted: SpawnIcon→ONS_CONTROLPOINT_BUILD on the CP; build completion→ONS_CONTROLPOINT_BUILT.

[onslaught.cp.icon_damage | presentation] Capture-icon damage / destroy mid-build (revert to neutral)  -  Audio emitted: shielded→ONS_DAMAGEBLOCKEDBYSHIELD to attacker (debounced); per-hit ONS_HIT1/2; destroy→ONS_GENERATOR_EXPLODE + ONSLAUGHT_CPDESTROYED_NONAME info. Presentation still missing: no EFFECT_SPARKS/ROCKET_EXPLODE particles, no progressive icon model swap.; Proximity self-destruct nearby-enemy reward MODELED (IconDamage attacker==self branch: ONS_TAKES+1/SCORE+5 to every nearby enemy within proximitydecap_distance), closed alongside onslaught.cp.proximity_decap.

[onslaught.cp.icon_think_regen | presentation] Built-icon steady-state slow regen  -  Damaged-FX EFFECT_ELECTRIC_SPARKS particle still not spawned (presentation/client). The ONS_SPARK1/2 ambient spark crackle IS now emitted: IconThink rolls random()<0.6-hp/max, then SPARK1 at random()>0.8 else SPARK2 at random()>0.5 (faithful branch structure).

[onslaught.gen.damage_destroy | presentation] Generator shield-gated damage + destruction  -  Audio emitted: blocked-by-shield hint; GENERATOR_UNDERATTACK center + ONS_GENERATOR_UNDERATTACK (10s debounce); per-hit ONS_GENERATOR_EXPLODE at chance damage/220 else ONS_HIT1/2; destroy→ONSLAUGHT_GENDESTROYED(_OVERTIME) info + ONS_GENERATOR_EXPLODE. Presentation still missing: progressive damage `frame=10*(1-hp/max)`, flaming-gib/EFFECT_SPARKS particles, ons_camSetup death-cam.; NOTE: the per-hit 'gib impact' branch maps QC SND_ROCKET_IMPACT to ONS_GENERATOR_EXPLODE; under-attack alarm uses PlayGlobal not play2team.

[onslaught.win.check_winner | presentation] Win check (last standing generator) + ST_ONS_GENS credit  -  BankRoundWin sends ROUND_TEAM_WIN_<team> center+info to all + play2all CTF_CAPTURE_<team> win jingle (winner>0), ROUND_TIED (winner==-1). Remaining: scoreboard column rendering + winner-banner HUD is a client concern (tracked under onslaught.presentation.csqc).

[onslaught.captureshield | presentation] CaptureShield (spinning shield model + push + blocked sound)  -  Server-side faithful + live: SpawnGenerator/SpawnControlPoint spawn a co-located SOLID_TRIGGER/MOVETYPE_NOCLIP ons_captureshield with the back-link (GtShieldFlag), EF_ADDITIVE, avelocity '7 0 11', hitbox 1.20x. ShieldTouchEntity reproduces ons_CaptureShield_Touch: pass-through for same-team or attackable/un-shielded node, else Combat.Damage(0, force=normalize(dir)*ShieldForce) push + play2 ONS_DAMAGEBLOCKEDBYSHIELD + GENERATOR/CONTROLPOINT_SHIELDED center.; presentation partial (client-deferred): the shield MODEL + EF_ADDITIVE additive blend + avelocity SPIN are assigned on the entity, but actually RENDERING the spinning additive shield is a CSQC/client concern not done here. ons_CaptureShield_Customize (per-viewer visibility) + ons_CaptureShield_Reset (colormap on map reset) not por

[onslaught.cp.proximity_decap | presentation] Proximity de-capture (stand near a point to flip it)  -  Proximity-decap IMPLEMENTED in IconThink: (friendly - enemy) live-player count within g_onslaught_cp_proximitydecap_distance changes icon HP by ±dps*THINKRATE each tick; at <=0 the icon is destroyed via IconDamage (lone enemy, or `self` to spread the multi-attacker +5 reward to all nearby enemies); a pure-friendly crowd repairs it. Master toggle defaults OFF in Base. Remaining: no waypoint/HUD presentation of the decap state.

[onslaught.presentation.csqc | logic,values,timing,presentation,audio,liveness] CSQC presentation (generator/icon models, animation, radar, death-cam, FX)  -  LANDED (W16/W17, server-authoritative + live-rendered): generator/control-point/icon WORLD MODELS now assigned (Entity.Model + EF_LOWPRECISION) and networked+rendered via the entity feed (ClientEntityView.ApplyIdentity, the CTF-flag pattern); progressive DAMAGE-MODEL swaps (GeneratorModelForBucket 0..10, IconModelForFraction 75/50/25%) + the pad PAD1->PAD2 capture swap; the generator damage FRAME (Frame=10*(1-hp/max)); the RADAR LINK lines round-trip (CollectRadarLinks -> SendRadarLinks -> ClientNet.RadarLinks -> RadarPanel.DrawLinkQuad, rendered); the generator/CP WAYPOINT SPRITES (CollectWaypoints -> SendWaypoints -> WaypointSpriteLayer/RadarPanel). Models resolved server-side approximate what CSQC generator_damage/cpicon_damage

[onslaught.audio.cues | presentation] Onslaught notifications + sounds emission  -  Core cues EMITTED live: SpawnIcon→ONS_CONTROLPOINT_BUILD; build completion→ONS_CONTROLPOINT_BUILT + ONSLAUGHT_CAPTURE_NONAME + ONS_CAPTURE(_TEAM)_NONAME; IconDamage→ONS_DAMAGEBLOCKEDBYSHIELD / ONS_HIT1/2 / ONS_GENERATOR_EXPLODE + ONSLAUGHT_CPDESTROYED_NONAME; GeneratorDamage→blocked-by-shield / GENERATOR_UNDERATTACK + ONS_GENERATOR_UNDERATTACK / ONS_HIT1-2 (or explode at chance damage/220) / ONSLAUGHT_GENDESTROYED(_OVERTIME) + explode; GeneratorThink→ONS_NOTSHIELDED_TEAM/_<team> + ONS_GENERATOR_ALARM (every 5s while un-shielded); OvertimeDecayTick first tick→OVERTIME_CONTROLPOINT + ONS_GENERATOR_DECAY; BankRoundWin→ROUND_TEAM_WIN / ROUND_TIED + play2all CTF_CAPTURE jingle. Sounds (7) + notifications (40) registered AND now all emitting.; Newly EMITTED this pass: ShieldTouchEntity→play2 ONS_DAMAGEBLOCKEDBY

[onslaught.gen.unshielded_alarm | presentation] Generator un-shielded periodic alarm (ons_GeneratorThink)  -  Presentation: the alarm is server-authoritative (center prints via NotificationSystem.Send + siren via SoundSystem); no client-side HUD/visual of the un-shielded state beyond the center-print. The siren is emitted once globally (SoundSystem.PlayGlobal) as the headless approximation of QC's per-player ATTEN_NONE soundto — no per-listener spatial routing.

[onslaught.bot.roles | timing] Bot Onslaught objective role (offense/defense/assistant goal rating)  -  Residual (logic simplifications, not partial-correctness): no waypoint-near-objective routing (Base navigation_routerating onto g_waypoints within 400 qu of the node + ++bestwp.cnt spread), no havocbot_attack_time (5s) throttle, no havocbot_goalrating_enemyplayers(20000,650) pass, and no havocbot_ons_target teammate-interest cost balancing. The decision FLOW (generator-attack first, skip CPs if a gen is rated, shield+neighbor-filter CPs, free-point ×2) is faithful.; timing partial: Base throttles re-rating via navigation_goalrating_timeout + havocbot_attack_time; the port re-rates every brain tick with no objective-attack cooldown.

[cl-crosshair.core.master_gating | logic] Master gating + enable/size/alpha early-outs  -  NARROWED: a dedicated UpdateCrosshairGate (NetGame:3980, called live at 2996) now reproduces the REACHABLE subset of Base's per-frame suppress-set (crosshair.qc:226-235): HEALTH<=0/dead (LocalDeadNow), intermission==2/GAME_STOPPED (MatchIntermission), scoreboard_active, spectatee_status==-1 (IsObserving) — the crosshair shows only when havePlayer AND none of these hold. STILL MISSING from the suppress-set: cl_lockview, CAMERA_SPECTATOR==2 dead-spectator mode, the viewloc/non-FREEAIM gate, the DrawCrosshair mutator hook, the minigame-menu gate, and hud!=HUD_NORMAL (vehicle handoff). Gate runs only where the skinned panel draws (host path); a pure remote client's reticle is owned by NetHud and gated separately.

[cl-crosshair.trueaim.showimpact | logic,presentation] Debug: draw crosshair at the reprojected impact point (crosshair_hittest_showimpact)  -  Base (crosshair.qc:306-321): the hit-test block reprojects the box-trace endpoint into screen space (wcross_origin) and, unless crosshair_hittest_showimpact is set, restores wcross_origin to the geometric centre. With showimpact=1 the crosshair is left drawn AT the projected impact point (a debug/aim-assist cue). The port always draws at the geometric panel centre (DrawPanel: center = Size2*0.5f) and never reprojects — the cvar is registered with the Base default (0) but has no reader.; Tied to the same missing screen-space reprojection as the trueaim HITOBSTRUCTION test; default 0 so the visible default behaviour matches Base.

[cl-crosshair.color.special_modes | logic,presentation] Crosshair color: weapon / health+armor / fixed (crosshair_color_special)  -  crosshair_color_special 3 (rainbow: randomvec()*brightness re-rolled every rainbow_delay, crosshair.qc:189-196) is not ported — ColorModeCvar (CrosshairPanel.cs:537-542) maps only 0/1/2, so mode 3 silently falls back to fixed color. The rainbow_brightness/delay cvars are registered but dead.

[cl-crosshair.anim.pickup_pulse | liveness] Item-pickup crosshair pulse (crosshair_pickup)  -  Host path only: UpdatePickupFeed calls CrosshairPanel.PulsePickup() (NetGame:3260) when a new weapon or a resource jump is detected on the LocalServerPlayer (re-baselined on respawn so the spawn loadout isn't counted). The crosshair bumps on item pickup on the host/listen-server. Whether server-spawned Solid.Not beam edicts are even included in the remote entity snapshot is unverified.; QC setattachment(beam, tur_head, "tag_fire") tag-socket attachment is not ported — the port approximates it by setting beam.Origin = muzzle shotOrg and beam.Angles = HeadWorldAngles each frame (no tag sub-entity in the port).; phaser.md3 head model is not attached as a separate tracking head bone — the head-frame animation drives the turret edict'

Base also nets TNSF_ANG to CSQC; the port has no turret CSQC net layer.

[turret-plasma_dual.presentation.head_frame_anim | presentation] Head-frame wheel animation (tr_think 0..6 + ++frame on fire)  -  Server-authoritative head-frame state (Entity.Frame) is now ported and live, but the CLIENT render of the spinning plasmad.md3 head bone is absent — the static base.md3 body has no spin frames and there is no tur_head sub-entity / CSQC turret net layer, so the wheel is not visibly drawn.; Two-barrel tag_fire alternation (tag_fire on plasmad.md3) not modeled — single computed ShotOrg muzzle.

[turret-tesla.identity.def | presentation] Identity, hitbox, models, spawnflags  -  Head model (tesla_head.md3) not instantiated as a separate head/coil entity; no tag_head attachment. TrThink computes head spin as state (HeadAngles) but grep confirms no head-bone node consumes HeadAngles, so the coil never visibly spins (see anim feature).

[turret-tesla.anim.tr_think | presentation] tr_think head spin-up + idle random crackle arc  -  Head spin not rendered: TrThink computes HeadAVelocity ('0 45 0' low-ammo / '0 180 0' charged, scaled by ammo/shot_dmg, zeroed when inactive) and integrates HeadAngles, but grep (2026-06-27) confirms no head-bone/coil entity (tesla_head.md3) consumes HeadAngles — the only readers are TurretAI track-motor/death-gib logic + TeslaTurret.TrThink itself. The coil never visibly spins; server-side state only. This is the last real gap on this unit.

The 'no per-level description string' gap from the 2026-06-22 baseline is CLOSED (Wave 8e, commit 412ca19): CampaignLevelDescription (GameWorld.cs) now reconstructs the description the SVQC slice drops.
